description: Industry features (contract analysis, invoice processing, compliance checking, PII detection, document classification, version comparison) and batch processing with progress tracking. These are the enterprise-grade capabilities.
steps: Create src/industry/document_classifier.py with DocumentClassifier class: Auto-classifies documents into types (CONTRACT, INVOICE, LEGAL, FINANCIAL, HR, MEDICAL, GENERAL). Methods: classify(text) -> (doc_type, confidence) uses keyword scoring and structural analysis, _keyword_scores(text) checks for type-indicative keywords (e.g., 'whereas', 'hereby' for contracts; 'invoice', 'total due' for invoices), _structural_analysis(text) checks formatting patterns (tables for invoices, numbered clauses for contracts). Returns top classification with confidence

Create src/industry/contract_analyzer.py with ContractAnalyzer class: Deep contract analysis. Methods: analyze(extraction_result) -> ContractAnalysis model, extract_clauses(text) identifies and categorizes clauses (termination, indemnity, confidentiality, force_majeure, limitation_of_liability, governing_law, payment, IP_rights), extract_obligations(text) -> List[Obligation] with party, description, deadline, track_expiry_dates(result) identifies key dates and computes days-until-expiry, calculate_risk_score(clauses, obligations) -> float scoring based on missing standard clauses, one-sided terms, unusual provisions. ContractAnalysis model includes: clauses, obligations, key_dates, risk_score, risk_factors, recommendations

Create src/industry/invoice_processor.py with InvoiceProcessor class: Invoice-specific extraction and validation. Methods: process(extraction_result) -> InvoiceData model, extract_line_items(text) parses tabular line items (description, quantity, unit_price, total), validate_amounts(line_items, total) cross-checks line item sum vs stated total, detect_duplicates(invoice_data, history) checks for duplicate invoice numbers, match_vendor(vendor_name, vendor_db) fuzzy matches against known vendors. InvoiceData model: vendor, invoice_number, date, due_date, line_items, subtotal, tax, total, currency, po_number, validation_status

Create src/industry/compliance_checker.py with ComplianceChecker class: Regulatory compliance analysis. Methods: check_compliance(document, doc_type, jurisdiction) -> ComplianceReport, _check_contract_compliance(text) verifies presence of required clauses (GDPR data processing, termination rights, governing law, dispute resolution), _check_gdpr_compliance(text) checks for data processing terms, retention periods, subject rights, _detect_missing_clauses(text, required_clauses) identifies gaps. ComplianceReport model: compliant (bool), findings: List[ComplianceFinding], missing_clauses, risk_level, recommendations

Create src/industry/pii_detector.py with PIIDetector class: PII detection and redaction. Methods: detect_pii(text) -> List[PIIEntity] identifies SSN, email, phone, credit card, address, DOB, passport numbers using regex + NER, redact(text, pii_entities, method='mask') replaces PII with [REDACTED] or masked versions, generate_pii_report(pii_entities) -> PIIReport with counts by type and risk assessment. PIIEntity model: type, value, start_pos, end_pos, confidence. Support configurable redaction methods: mask, hash, remove

Create src/industry/version_comparator.py with VersionComparator class: Compare document versions. Methods: compare(doc_v1, doc_v2) -> ComparisonResult, _diff_text(text1, text2) using difflib for text-level differences, _diff_clauses(clauses1, clauses2) for semantic clause comparison, _identify_changes(diffs) categorizes as added/removed/modified, _assess_significance(changes) rates change importance. ComparisonResult model: changes (List[Change]), summary, added_clauses, removed_clauses, modified_clauses, significance_score

Create src/batch/progress_tracker.py with ProgressTracker class: Track batch job progress. Methods: create_job(job_id, total_items) -> BatchJob, update_progress(job_id, item_id, status), get_progress(job_id) -> BatchProgress, mark_complete(job_id), mark_failed(job_id, error). BatchJob model: id, status, total_items, completed_items, failed_items, start_time, end_time, progress_pct. Store state in SQLite for persistence across restarts

Create src/batch/batch_processor.py with BatchProcessor class: Parallel document processing. Methods: async process_batch(file_paths, mode, options) -> BatchResult using asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency control (max from config), _process_single(file_path, mode, semaphore, tracker) wraps individual document processing with error handling and progress updates, get_batch_status(job_id) returns current progress, cancel_batch(job_id) sets cancellation flag. Integrates with ProgressTracker and DocumentAgent orchestrator
acceptance_criteria: DocumentClassifier correctly classifies contract-like text as CONTRACT with >0.7 confidence
ContractAnalyzer extracts clauses and computes risk scores from contract text
InvoiceProcessor extracts line items and validates totals
PIIDetector finds and redacts SSN, email, phone patterns in text
VersionComparator produces meaningful diffs between two text versions
BatchProcessor handles multiple documents concurrently with progress tracking
ProgressTracker persists job state and reports accurate progress percentages
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "from src.industry.document_classifier import DocumentClassifier; dc = DocumentClassifier(); result = dc.classify('This Agreement is entered into by and between Party A and Party B, whereas...'); print(f'Classification: {result}')"
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "from src.industry.contract_analyzer import ContractAnalyzer; print('ContractAnalyzer OK')"

Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "from src.industry.pii_detector import PIIDetector; d = PIIDetector(); result = d.detect_pii('Contact john@example.com or call 555-123-4567'); print(f'Found {len(result)} PII entities')"
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "from src.batch.batch_processor import BatchProcessor; print('BatchProcessor OK')"
